attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who targets stage wins and GC from early moves rather than late attacks. Willing to ride aggressively against more experienced climbers to achieve objectives.
team_tactics: Groupama-FDJ provides breakaway positioning but limited protection in crosswinds or high-tempo chase scenarios. Rider appears to operate with independence once in the break.
terrain_preference: Suited to moderate climbs and breakaway scenarios with quality riders. Exposed on steep gradients when pace exceeds threshold and in crosswind/echelon situations despite team presence.
vulnerability: Struggles when climbing pace lifts beyond threshold on steep gradients (lost 1:09 in final 3km). Poor positioning awareness in crosswind splits despite available team support.
key_patterns: Targets GC and stages from breakaways rather than peloton-based racing (2 observations)
Shows aggressive racing mentality despite youth, unwilling to cooperate passively with veterans (2 observations)
Vulnerable to high-pace attrition: crosswinds and sustained steep climbing both exposed weaknesses (2 observations)
Can survive quality breakaways when pace is controlled but cracks when intensity spikes (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: After becoming youngest GT leader since 1904, immediately suffered crosswind isolation and climbing losses when pace intensified, suggesting initial success was opportunity-based rather than form-based
